tldr: Headlines split between the fragility of the US-Iran ceasefire as a security event and the cascading economic damage from a closed Strait of Hormuz, with a secondary thread on the diplomatic politics of reopening.

silences.note: One of two Yonhap/KBS headlines is entirely domestic (special counsel sentencing of ex-President Yoon); the other covers Hormuz ship debris. Only the debris headline is on-topic; the Yoon headline contains zero Hormuz framing.
Both SCMP headlines cover the Wall Street 'Taco'/'Nacho' nickname story for Trump's China visit — economic-commentary framing, not Hormuz coverage.

bottom_line: The headline layer reads the Hormuz crisis through two lenses simultaneously: a military standoff with a fragile ceasefire, and a commodity-supply shock draining oil reserves. The diplomatic and policy angles are real but secondary.
